facade_description: 34 Oxford St presents a bay-and-gable domestic facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance. HCD note: Each property has value as a late-nineteenth- to early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian and early-twentieth-century architectural styles.
